ground_truth.title: Discuss the VirtualBox 7.1.0 release here
ground_truth.author: galitsyn
ground_truth.main_content:
galitsyn
11. Sep 2024, 18:36

You can download the release here.
VirtualBox 7.1.0 is a significant new version.

Changelog is here.

Simple07
11. Sep 2024, 21:06

Having upgraded from 7.1.0 Beta 2 to 7.1.0 build 164728 GA release, I am having network issues.
Tests underway.

ehasis
11. Sep 2024, 22:05

I'm also having network problems. Not working with NAT on Windows Host (11) with Windows Guests (XP and 7). Updated Guest additions on both guests.

multiOS
11. Sep 2024, 23:05

Also seeing same problem after installing 7.1.0, i.e. loss of Guests' internet access. Returning to the 7.1.0 BETA 2 restores internet access. (Presumably re-installing 7.0.20 or whichever previous version was used will do the same).

linuxnerd
11. Sep 2024, 23:14

Running on Windows 11 here.
NAT networking works but giving horrendous performance.
Getting pings of over 400ms to 10.0.2.2.
Please fix this ASAP.

multiOS
12. Sep 2024, 00:37

There are further reports of the same/similar issue here: viewtopic.php?t=112323

ant
12. Sep 2024, 00:38

Same here in my updated (with yesterday's updates), 64-bit W10 Pro. PC. Both my old, updated 64-bit W7 HPE SP1 and updated 64-bit W11 Home 23H2 guest VMs have the same problems. It didn't have matter if it has the v7.0.20 and v7.1 additional tools too. I also rebooted my host. No changes. I attached my VB7.0.20and7.1LogsFrom64w7hpeSP1guestVMon64w10pro.7z. They including v7.0.20 and v7.1 logs to compare. I am going to reinstall v7.0.20.

Guys, please do include your logs since the support will want them to fix this quickly.

kabu
12. Sep 2024, 08:42

Created new bug report for NAT issue here
https://www.virtualbox.org/ticket/22164

Stickybit
12. Sep 2024, 09:41

Seeing the slow IPv4 NAT issue as well.

(killed the VM at "00:02:12.162465 Changing the VM state from 'RUNNING' to 'SUSPENDING'" while waiting for slow network)

Reverting to 7.0.20 and NAT is all good.

Best regards, Stickybit

FranceBB
12. Sep 2024, 09:59

Bug with ticket number 22123 about VBoxTray.exe high CPU usage on XP x86 confirmed on 7.1.0 r164728 Stable: https://www.virtualbox.org/ticket/22123

Logs in attachment.

DevOpsUser
12. Sep 2024, 10:57

Extremely slow network performance with VirtualBox 7.1.0 in all our Linux machines (Ubuntu 24.04, Debian 12, etc. pp.) detected here too.

A simple apt-get upgrade now takes hours to download new packages. Downloads with a few bytes per second!

We had to downgrade to 7.0.20 and everything is back to normal, so obviously there's a serious regression in jump from 7.0.x to 7.1.0

ant
12. Sep 2024, 12:44

kabu wrote:
Created new bug report for NAT issue here
https://www.virtualbox.org/ticket/22164

Awesome and thanks.

awakened
13. Sep 2024, 09:28

This NAT issue was already present in BETA 2 (see viewtopic.php?t=112307 ), amazing this 7.1.0 was actually released without even addressing that issue.
What's up with the release management? It is very poor since 7+, former release even crashed hosts!

BulentS
13. Sep 2024, 12:21

I have a problem with NAT.
I use Arch Linux on host and Windows 10 on guest.
My routing table on the host also has the definition
169.254.12.0/24 dev eth0 proto static scope link metric 90

While I can access 169.254.12.xx addresses from the guest with Virtualbox 7.0.x, I cannot do this with Virtualbox 7.1.0.
Regards,
Bulent

JustinH
13. Sep 2024, 16:19

Why does the Oracle site not advertise this new version? Is there always a lag between the VB site and the Oracle site?

https://www.oracle.com/virtualization/virtualbox/
with: VirtualBox 7.1.0 is a significant new version.
Having upgraded from 7.1.0 Beta 2 to 7.1.0 build 164728 GA release, I am having network issues.
I'm also having network problems. Not working with NAT on Windows Host (11) with Windows Guests (XP and 7).
NAT networking works but giving horrendous performance.
Extremely slow network performance with VirtualBox 7.1.0 in all our Linux machines (Ubuntu 24.04, Debian 12, etc. pp.) detected here too.
We had to downgrade to 7.0.20 and everything is back to normal, so obviously there's a serious regression in jump from 7.0.x to 7.1.0
This NAT issue was already present in BETA 2
Created new bug report for NAT issue here
